title: Gagea soleimani
taxonomy: Gagea soleimani is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Liliaceae and the genus Gagea. The genus Gagea is a group of flowering plants commonly known as adder tongues. Within the family Liliaceae, Gagea soleimani is further classified as a member of the order Asparagales. The order Asparagales is a large group of flowering plants that also includes the families Agapanthaceae and Asparagaceae. Further classification of Gagea soleimani at the species level is typically based on its unique morphological characteristics and geographic distribution.

intro: Gagea soleimani, commonly known as Soleiman's Gagea, is a perennial plant species in the family Liliaceae. This plant has been reported to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential use in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Gagea soleimani include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Gagea soleimani is often used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat conditions like digestive issues and skin problems.
